title: Tribute To The Vanquished.
content:
("Laissez-moi pleurer sur cette race.")

    [I. v.]


    Oh! let me weep that race whose day is past,
    By exile given, by exile claimed once more,
    Thrice swept away upon that fatal blast.
    Whate'er its blame, escort we to our shore
    These relics of the monarchy of yore;
    And to th' outmarching oriflamme be paid
    War's honors by the flag on Fleurus' field displayed!

    Fraser's Magazine
meaning:
This evocative piece by Victor-Marie Hugo, titled "Tribute To The Vanquished.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
